topic.what: SSG (Sort-then-Split by Groups): a logit-balanced vocabulary partitioning algorithm for LLM watermarking that improves watermark detectability in low-entropy settings
topic.how: partitions vocabulary into logit-balanced green/red sets by sorting tokens by logit, grouping consecutive pairs, and hash-based assignment within groups
topic.why_matters: KGW watermarking degrades significantly in low-entropy tasks (code/math generation) where next-token distribution is sharply peaked; SSG guarantees higher lower bound on watermark strength without compromising text quality

characteristics.what_it_measures: True positive rate at fixed false positive rate thresholds
characteristics.vs_baseline: KGW+SSG vs KGW: TPR@1 13.4%→14.0% (LLaMA HumanEval); 41.7%→90.9% (DSMath GSM8K)
characteristics.context: SSG boosts TPR significantly on low-entropy tasks across all base methods (KGW, SWEET, EWD)

characteristics.what_it_measures: F1 score at 1% and 5% FPR thresholds
characteristics.context: Consistent F1 improvements across models and datasets for low-entropy tasks

characteristics.what_it_measures: Token-level normalized probability shift toward green set due to bias
characteristics.vs_baseline: KGW: near-zero strength tokens common; SSG: enforces lower bound based on top token probability p1
characteristics.context: SSG reshapes watermark strength distribution reducing near-zero tokens; theoretical guarantee scales with probability concentration

characteristics.what_it_measures: Pass@1 accuracy for code generation tasks
characteristics.vs_baseline: Similar to baseline methods; SSG does not significantly degrade quality
characteristics.context: Pass@1 comparable to KGW/SWEET/EWD without SSG; watermarking causes inherent quality reduction but SSG does not add further degradation

characteristics.what_it_measures: Token generation throughput relative to baseline
characteristics.vs_baseline: EWD baseline normalized to 100%; k=2 incurs minimal overhead
characteristics.context: k=2 or k=4 recommended; larger k adds computational overhead without proportional detection gains

characteristics.what_it_measures: False positive rate at which TPR is measured
characteristics.context: Evaluated at 1% and 5% FPR consistent with prior watermarking literature
